text: Is it possible that we have technology enough to build a personal ultralight aircraft that can lift and fly 90kg around without combustion or propellers? I've been looking at Dyson Fans and MIT's Ionic Propulsion and the latest battery technology. What do you think?

replies.author: @patomahony9747
replies.text:
Why not we currently have drones delivering 20 kg payloads. In uae there are autonomous drone taxis and airbus have a system of attaching wings to a smart car at a hub and flying to its other hub in next city where wings clip off and you drive off.
Back in 90’s there were pedal powered gliders being experimented with so add an ebike to the equation with the lighter modern day battery and experiment to get the best set up

text: I like the video though I must admit that I’m a pacifist and never liked Shakespeare because there are always casualties. At least that is my understanding of what the man was writing. I’m not English, neither is my parental language., Whatever I  believe this kind of thinking has caused a lot of suffering around the world. As did all colonial countries/governments. I think it’s important to state that we’re worldwide citizens who all have common needs, wishes and with a common thrive we can get beyond differences. Let’s work together, the Middle Ages are hopefully long gone.

Awesome Robert! It's fun to learn about the untold (or not well disseminated) side of history and/or the often surprising connection between people/events in history.  There was a show on BBC like this long time ago - but can't recall the name. (Anyone remember the name?)
Couple of thoughts:

- Also, please consider doing a video about iron batteries and maybe building one, as they seem to be all the rage these days and do NOT blow up ;)

author: @amb3cog
text: Sounds like the big issue was wind, and volume level. I'm sure both can be easily solved. Anything that has noise canceling microphones, like a quality cell phone, will take care of the wind. I'm not so sure about the volume. But I know you can usually increase volume/level with a preamp, or something along those lines. You might even be able to do it in software nowadays. But my main point is you want a noise canceling mic, which is actually multiple mics. So two (or more) mics, and the differences between the two will be rejected, thereby canceling the wind noise. Only your clean voice will remain. ✌️

replies.author: @amb3cog
replies.text: Umm, an old iPhone would probably work now that I think about it. And usually someone has one floating around. If the battery is dead. Get a battery pack for it. Preferably one with a lot of amperage. Or try to replace it. ✌️

replies.author: @ThinkingandTinkering
replies.text: agreed mate - I have a zoom h2n with a wind sock I am going to try that I think an maybe equalisation in post production?

replies.author: @amb3cog
replies.text:
@ThinkingandTinkering There you go. That sounds like a plan. Especially since it won't cost anything. I'm very curious to see how this works out for you. I've never used a mic with a wind sock like that. I just know from talking to people with cheap phones, and people with quality phones that quality phones with the multiple/noise canceling mics sound night and day different. 

This is one of those things that very few people in the general public seem to understand it seems. At least that's my experience, especially answering phones at work and dealing with this issue. People get all frustrated at us for not being able to understand them, and it's really funny to me. Because the fault lies in the cheap Walmart/Amazon special phone they're using, not our expensive land line phone system. And/or where they're physically located at. Especially since we're in an enclosed structure with a regulated environment. But good luck explaining that to Joe Average. 🙄 😁✌️
